title: Thelypteris thelypteris
taxonomy: Thelypteris thelypteris is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae. This species is a type of fern, specifically a member of the genus Thelypteris. Further classification places it within the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. The order Polypodiales is a large group of ferns that also includes the families Dryopteridaceae and Blechnaceae. Thelypteris thelypteris is often considered a synonym of the species Cyclosorus thelypteris, which is a common name for this species.
uses: Thelypteris thelypteris, commonly known as the maiden fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and rhizomes are often employed in herbal remedies to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation. In some cultures, the maiden fern is used to treat fever and colds.
